annotation_evidence: local RCSB PDB COMPND molecule and chain records
local RCSB PDB REMARK 350 author-determined biological assemblies
historically selected target and binder chain IDs

policy.semantic_pass_definition: The selected chains are distinct biological interaction partners that form a defensible target-binder-like system rather than internal subunits of a larger obligate assembly.

targets.rationale: The selected histone H3 and H4 chains are internal subunits of a decameric nucleosome assembly, not a standalone target-binder system.
Both selected chains are DCoH molecules within a tetrameric HNF-1/DCoH complex rather than distinct target and binder entities.
The selected S6 and S18 chains are internal components of a 30S ribosomal assembly.
Both selected chains are GRB2 SH2 domains in a domain-swapped tetramer that also contains receptor peptides.
Both selected chains are VEGF molecules from a tetrameric VEGF-receptor assembly; the receptor chains are omitted.
The selected FSH alpha and beta chains form a complete natural hormone heterodimer, but neither chain is a target-directed binder in the strict successor regime.
Both selected chains are holo-ACP synthase subunits inside a hexameric assembly rather than an enzyme-carrier target-binder pair.
The selected chains are the internal heavy and light variable domains of one antibody Fv, not an antibody-antigen target-binder pair.
The selected chains are the heavy and light variable domains of an Fv embedded in an eicosameric cytochrome bc1-antibody assembly.
The selected CheY and CheA domains are distinct interacting proteins in a complete author-determined dimer.
The selected colicin E9 DNase and Im9 immunity protein are distinct target-binder-like partners in a complete author-determined dimer.
The selected L14 and L24E chains are internal components of a large ribosomal assembly.
The selected chains are prefoldin subunits inside a hexameric chaperone assembly.
The selected alpha and beta globin chains are internal subunits of tetrameric hemoglobin.
The selected gamma and alpha globin chains are internal subunits of tetrameric fetal hemoglobin.
The selected beta and alpha globin chains are internal subunits of tetrameric hemoglobin.
The selected beta and alpha globin chains are internal subunits of tetrameric hemoglobin.
Both selected chains are RAD50 ATPase fragments in a tetrameric construct rather than distinct target and binder proteins.
The selected TRAF2 and TRADD domains are distinct interacting proteins in a complete author-determined dimer.
The selected phycocyanin chains are internal subunits of a dodecameric photosynthetic assembly.
The selected phosphorelay proteins are biologically meaningful interaction partners, but their author-determined assembly contains four protein chains rather than the isolated selected pair.
The selected MHC alpha and beta chains are internal receptor subunits and the bound peptide chain is omitted.
FGFR1 and FGF2 are meaningful interaction partners, but the biological assembly is a tetrameric receptor-ligand-heparin complex and the selected pair is incomplete.
The allergen and antibody heavy chain are meaningful partners, but the trimeric Fab-antigen assembly also requires the omitted antibody light chain.
